what_it_does: This file is the single-page HTML shell for a design-system gallery: it renders a navigable, versioned collection of design pieces in a two-pane desktop layout (rail + stage) and a work/panel split on mobile, with inline CSS for the chrome, comment pins, compare mode, warmth dial, and three off-canvas drawers (Tokens, Grammar, Layouts), plus script tags that load the shared surface runtime and gallery modules.
observations: Large inline stylesheet using CSS custom properties generated from tokens/grammar.
Three fixed drawers: Tokens on the right, Grammar on the left, Layouts on the right, each namespaced to avoid palette-variable collisions.
Mobile-specific layer (≤920px) transforms the layout into a work/panel split with a draggable divider, panel fill selector, zoom controls, and a hidden skim strip.
Comment overlay with pins and popups positioned over the iframe preview stage.
Version switcher and compare bar for before/after version selection.
Bottom script wiring loads surface runtime, gallery modules, rooms shell, bench/map/almanac, and a vee-dock component.
Inline comments include mobile refactor flags (mob-XX) and attributions (Tim).
Logic is delegated to external JS modules; the HTML is mostly chrome and wiring.
standouts: The three drawer namespaces (--ui-*, --gm-*, and bare surface vars) isolate chrome palettes from each other and from the mockup palette.
Mobile-native viewer split with draggable joint and panel-fill routing.
Live-driven design tokens and generative grammar preview embedded directly in the gallery UI.
conventions: CSS custom properties from generated surface.css.
BEM-ish but loose class naming with semantic names.
External JS modules for behavior; HTML is shell/wiring.
Responsive breakpoints and coarse-pointer media queries.
Inline documentation comments explaining design decisions.
concerns: Very large inline stylesheet mixing layout, components, drawers, and mobile overrides in one file.
Duplicate .item CSS blocks with conflicting padding/margin values.
Inline on* handlers and global functions assumed from modules create coupling not visible here.
Mobile and desktop rules interleaved; high specificity risk.
Comments reference external files (dna/tokens.json, dna/grammar.json, dna/layouts.json, dna/application.json) not shown in this file.
notes: The gallery.html is the chrome shell: it declares the visual architecture, loads generated design tokens, and delegates all data/behavior to the surface runtime and gallery modules.
questions: Where is the actual piece registry loaded from (buildList, DNA.gallery.init)?
How are the iframe src URLs resolved for each piece/version?
What is the role of DNA.mountAtlas and the rooms/bench/map/almanac scripts on this page?
Are the duplicate .item CSS blocks intentional or leftover from a refactor?
How do the three drawers avoid z-index/stacking conflicts when two are open?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a versioned design gallery, and the file indeed implements the chrome, navigation, preview stage, compare, feedback, token/grammar/layout drawers, and mobile split viewer for that purpose.
intention_signals: It is trying to be a unified review environment where designers can browse versioned pieces, compare versions, annotate the work, and tune the underlying design tokens, grammar, and layouts in real time, with a polished mobile experience.
novelty: The combination of a versioned gallery, live token/grammar/layout drawers, and a mobile-specific work/panel split with draggable divider and panel-fill routing in a single static HTML shell.
contribution: A complete, self-contained HTML/CSS chrome shell for a design-system gallery that demonstrates how generated tokens, grammar, layouts, and review tools can be composed into one surface; the drawer namespace pattern and mobile split-view wiring are reusable.
summary_for_embedding: A single-page HTML shell for the Design DNA gallery, providing a token-styled, versioned piece browser with rail navigation, iframe stage, compare/comment/feedback tools, and off-canvas Tokens, Grammar, and Layouts drawers, plus a mobile work/panel split viewer.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the central review surface where generated design-system artifacts are browsed, compared, annotated, and tuned in real time across desktop and mobile.
